text:
This is a Public-API review. The Mathlib dependencies (`GroupLike` with its `CommMonoid` instance, `Algebra.IsGeometricallyReduced`, `Algebra.FiniteType`) all check out. My findings concern the exposed surface.

TAUCETI-VERDICT-1af9036ffb6d53233fd0e2b3

```json
{
  "verdict": "request_changes",
  "summary": "The public class hierarchy exposes vacuous `True` fields, a content-free `True` lemma, and an unused `AlgPoints` definition with no API; the `IsUnipotent` characteristic API is also incomplete. The exposed interface either characterizes nothing or has no consumer.",
  "findings": [
    {
      "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean",
      "line": "62",
      "issue": "`ReductiveGroup.unipotent_radical_trivial : True` and `SemisimpleGroup.radical_trivial : True` are vacuous fields. The classes are therefore definitionally `AffineAlgGroup` plus no constraint, so `ReductiveGroup`/`SemisimpleGroup` claim a characterization they do not encode — a misleading public interface (every affine algebraic group is 'reductive').",
      "fix": "Do not expose `ReductiveGroup`/`SemisimpleGroup` until the unipotent-radical predicate exists; or carry the real predicate as the field. Exposing `True`-fielded classes as the public hierarchy now ships a placeholder masquerading as the definition.",
      "evidence": "`unipotent_radical_trivial : True`, `radical_trivial : True` in the diff."
    },
    {
      "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean",
      "line": "73",
      "issue": "`ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial'` is a public theorem of type `True`. It restates a `True` field and carries no content; it pollutes the API surface.",
      "fix": "Remove the lemma. A consumer gains nothing from a `True`-valued restatement.",
      "evidence": "`theorem ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial' ... : True := h.unipotent_radical_trivial`."
    },
    {
      "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean",
      "line": "35",
      "issue": "`AlgPoints` is exposed but has no consumer in this PR (`IsUnipotent` operates on `GroupLike k A`, not `AlgPoints`) and ships with zero characteristic API — no coercion, no `mem`/ext lemmas. It is orphan public surface.",
      "fix": "Drop `AlgPoints` until a downstream target uses it, or make it `private`; if kept, give it the `*_def`/coercion lemmas a thin wrapper needs.",
      "evidence": "`def AlgPoints ... := A →ₐ[k] k`; no other reference to `AlgPoints` in the diff."
    },
    {
      "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean",
      "line": "44",
      "issue": "`IsUnipotent`'s characteristic API is incomplete: only `isUnipotent_one` (introduction) is provided. There is no `isUnipotent_def`/`isUnipotent_iff` restatement, so consumers must unfold to `IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)`.",
      "fix": "Add `isUnipotent_iff : IsUnipotent g ↔ IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1) := Iff.rfl` (or `_def`) so the predicate can be used without unfolding.",
      "evidence": "`def IsUnipotent (g : GroupLike k A) : Prop := IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)` with only `isUnipotent_one` exposed."
    },
    {
      "file": "TauCeti/AlgebraicGroup/Reductive.lean",
      "line": "78",
      "issue": "`SemisimpleGroup.instReductiveGroup` duplicates the parent-projection instance that `class SemisimpleGroup extends ReductiveGroup` already generates (`SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup`), adding a redundant instance to the surface.",
      "fix": "Remove the explicit instance; the `extends` clause already provides the `SemisimpleGroup → ReductiveGroup` instance.",
      "evidence": "`class SemisimpleGroup ... extends ReductiveGroup k A` plus the manual `instance ... := SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup`."
    }
  ]
}
```

verdict_obj.summary: The public class hierarchy exposes vacuous `True` fields, a content-free `True` lemma, and an unused `AlgPoints` definition with no API; the `IsUnipotent` characteristic API is also incomplete. The exposed interface either characterizes nothing or has no consumer.

findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup.unipotent_radical_trivial : True` and `SemisimpleGroup.radical_trivial : True` are vacuous fields. The classes are therefore definitionally `AffineAlgGroup` plus no constraint, so `ReductiveGroup`/`SemisimpleGroup` claim a characterization they do not encode — a misleading public interface (every affine algebraic group is 'reductive').
findings.fix: Do not expose `ReductiveGroup`/`SemisimpleGroup` until the unipotent-radical predicate exists; or carry the real predicate as the field. Exposing `True`-fielded classes as the public hierarchy now ships a placeholder masquerading as the definition.

findings.issue: `ReductiveGroup.unipotentRadical_trivial'` is a public theorem of type `True`. It restates a `True` field and carries no content; it pollutes the API surface.
findings.fix: Remove the lemma. A consumer gains nothing from a `True`-valued restatement.

findings.issue: `AlgPoints` is exposed but has no consumer in this PR (`IsUnipotent` operates on `GroupLike k A`, not `AlgPoints`) and ships with zero characteristic API — no coercion, no `mem`/ext lemmas. It is orphan public surface.
findings.fix: Drop `AlgPoints` until a downstream target uses it, or make it `private`; if kept, give it the `*_def`/coercion lemmas a thin wrapper needs.
findings.evidence: `def AlgPoints ... := A →ₐ[k] k`; no other reference to `AlgPoints` in the diff.

findings.issue: `IsUnipotent`'s characteristic API is incomplete: only `isUnipotent_one` (introduction) is provided. There is no `isUnipotent_def`/`isUnipotent_iff` restatement, so consumers must unfold to `IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)`.
findings.fix: Add `isUnipotent_iff : IsUnipotent g ↔ IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1) := Iff.rfl` (or `_def`) so the predicate can be used without unfolding.
findings.evidence: `def IsUnipotent (g : GroupLike k A) : Prop := IsNilpotent ((g : A) - 1)` with only `isUnipotent_one` exposed.

findings.issue: `SemisimpleGroup.instReductiveGroup` duplicates the parent-projection instance that `class SemisimpleGroup extends ReductiveGroup` already generates (`SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup`), adding a redundant instance to the surface.
findings.fix: Remove the explicit instance; the `extends` clause already provides the `SemisimpleGroup → ReductiveGroup` instance.
findings.evidence: `class SemisimpleGroup ... extends ReductiveGroup k A` plus the manual `instance ... := SemisimpleGroup.toReductiveGroup`.
